macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Major Indices Show Sideways Drift with Limited Volatility Ahead of Fed Minutes
items.summary: The S&P 500 (SPY), Nasdaq 100 (QQQ), and Russell 2000 (IWM) entered a low-volume float with sideways, drifting action following Thursday's sharp decline, while the Nasdaq 100 specifically rose 1.26% and the Russell 2000 gained 0.45%[1][3].

items.title: Fed Minutes and Waller's Forward Guidance Comments Dominate Central Bank News
items.summary: The FOMC minutes are scheduled for release on Wednesday, July 8, during market hours, while Fed Governor Waller stated that forward guidance can be a 'valuable tool' but may become a hindrance[1][2][3].

items.title: OPEC+ Agrees to Increase Oil Production by 188,000 Barrels Daily in August
items.summary: OPEC+ announced that seven member countries will expand crude oil production by a combined 188,000 barrels per day in August as exports through the Strait of Hormuz recover amid a US-Iran ceasefire[4][6].

items.title: Moderna and Broadcom Lead Stock Market Gains with Significant Rally
items.summary: Moderna (MRNA) surged over 30% in six trading sessions (up 41.45% since June 25) due to vaccine developments, while Broadcom (AVGO) recovered 4%+ to reclaim critical intraday levels[3].

items.title: Tesla Rebounds 6.69% Following Thursday's Sharp Decline
items.summary: Tesla regained 6.69% of its decline from Thursday, marking a notable recovery in the broader market's sideways drift[3].

items.title: Goldman Sachs Warns AI Could Displace 9% of U.S. Workforce
items.summary: Goldman Sachs released a report stating that artificial intelligence could displace 9% of the U.S. workforce, adding to macroeconomic concerns about labor market volatility[2].

items.title: Gold and Silver Prices Rise Amid Market Uncertainty
items.summary: Gold futures opened at $4,187.50 per troy ounce, up 1.5% from the previous week, while silver rose 2.33% and gold gained 1.23% in the latest session[1][4].

items.title: SpaceX Joins Nasdaq 100 Following Recent IPO
items.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) officially joined the Nasdaq 100 index on Tuesday after going public via an IPO three weeks prior, marking a significant addition to the tech-heavy index[8].

items.title: Delta Air Lines to Kick Off Airline Earnings Season on July 10
items.summary: Delta Air Lines (DAL) is set to report earnings this Friday, July 10, as airlines face a balancing act between capacity and elevated jet fuel costs that remain 20% higher than a year ago despite lower oil prices[7].

items.title: Samsung Electronics Reports Preliminary 19-Fold Profit Jump in Q2
items.summary: Samsung Electronics released preliminary second-quarter earnings showing a 19-fold profit increase from the previous year, signaling strong performance in the semiconductor sector[5].

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Strategy Sells $216M Bitcoin to Fund Shareholder Dividends
items.summary: MicroStrategy (formerly Strategy) sold $216 million in Bitcoin to fund its shareholder dividends, marking a shift in its crypto treasury strategy after announcing a new framework last week.

items.title: Tom Lee's BitMine Buys 42,197 ETH Amid Clarity Act Boost
items.summary: Tom Lee's BitMine added 74 million in Ether (42,197 ETH) as it bets on the Clarity Act's potential to boost Ethereum, while Ethereum underperformed the market by roughly 40% before starting to rise.

items.title: Nearly 1 Million Investors Lose $3.81B on Trump Meme Coin
items.summary: A New York Times report citing Nansen analytics reveals that nearly one million people who invested in President Trump's meme coin lost a total of $3.81 billion, while insiders who bought under $1 remain profitable.

items.title: Trump Profits $636M from Crypto Ventures Despite Retail Losses
items.summary: President Trump's financial disclosure shows he walked away with a $636 million payout from his crypto bet, part of a $2.2 billion haul from all business ventures in 2025, despite retail investors losing billions.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin Announces Major Ethereum Overhaul for Quantum Safety
items.summary: Vitalik Buterin unveiled a new Ethereum roadmap involving a 3–4 year protocol rebuild to prioritize quantum safety and privacy, with nearly every core piece of the protocol set to be rebuilt.

items.title: UK Bans Crypto Donations to Political Parties to Curb Foreign Influence
items.summary: The UK government has banned cryptocurrency donations to political parties in an effort to prevent foreign influence in domestic politics, following scrutiny over Trump's crypto income.

items.title: Coinbase World Cup Alert Error Highlights Prediction Market Proof Issues
items.summary: A disputed Coinbase sports alert during the World Cup exposed critical flaws in exchange-run prediction markets, underscoring the need for verified event data and clear accountability before scaling automated content.

items.title: Crypto Banter Analyst Predicts Bitcoin Push to $70K if Support Holds
items.summary: Crypto Banter analyst suggests Bitcoin could surge into the $70,000s if it maintains support above $57,000, though mid-timeframe bearish divergence hints at potential downside before the breakout.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: AMD Launches Ryzen AI Halo: A $4,000 Developer Machine Competing with NVIDIA's Tiny AI Box
items.summary: AMD has released the Ryzen AI Halo, a $4,000 developer machine that outperforms NVIDIA's Spark in token generation across multiple models, including GLM Flash 30B (14% faster) and Quen 3.51 122B (12% faster), while also delivering image generation speeds up to seven times faster than Apple M4 Pro.

items.title: Google Cloud Introduces Fable 5 Model with Dynamic Workflows for Intent-Driven Software Development
items.summary: Google Cloud has launched the Fable 5 model, enabling intent-driven development where users tell AI what to build rather than how; the new Dynamic Workflows feature spawns parallel engineering subagents to generate modular UI, audio, and haptics in real time.

items.title: Houdini 22 Adds 'Character Picker' Button, Signaling Near Release of AI-Enhanced Animation Tools
items.summary: A button labeled 'Character Picker' has appeared in Houdini 22, indicating that AI-driven animation workflow tools are imminent, following recent advancements in procedural skinning, dynamic proxy animation, and machine learning integration with Apex scripting.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Gunvolt Chronicles: Luminous Avenger iX 1+2 Dual Collection and Pel World 1.0 Launch This Week
items.summary: Notable indie and major releases include the Gunvolt Chronicles bundle, co-op horror Forest Escape: Last Train, vampire life sim Moonlight Peaks, and the open-world survival game Pel World hitting its final 1.0 version after early access controversy.

items.title: Pel World 1.0 Final Release Marks Major Milestone for Open-World Survival Crafter
items.summary: Pel World, the mega-hit open-world survival crafting game that launched in early access with internet-wide attention and controversy, officially releases its 1.0 final version, marking a significant industry development.

items.title: FIFA Suspends USA Striker Folarin Balogun's One-Game Ban After Trump Intervention
items.summary: In a major esports and sports governance development, FIFA overturned a red card suspension for USA striker Folarin Balogun following direct intervention by President Trump, suspending his one-game ban.

items.title: Serral's New StarCraft 2 Patch Makes Brood Lord Strategy Insanely Strong
items.summary: Top StarCraft 2 player Serral reveals that the latest game patch has made the Brood Lord strategy exceptionally powerful, signaling a major shift in competitive esports tactics for the military sci-fi RTS.

items.title: Smite 2 Competitive Guide Released for 2026 Season
items.summary: A comprehensive guide on how to improve at Smite 2 in 2026 has been published, featuring updated builds, tier lists, and account strategies for the competitive gaming community.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Samsung Unveils Galaxy Z TriFold as Most Advanced Foldable Yet
items.summary: Samsung debuted its Galaxy Z TriFold, featuring a redesigned dual-hinge system, upgraded materials, and a 10-inch display that unfolds from a pocketable frame, marking its most advanced foldable device.

items.title: Form Energy Secures $405M for 100-Hour Battery Development
items.summary: Form Energy announced $405 million in funding to accelerate the development of its long-awaited 100-hour battery, a longer-lasting alternative to lithium batteries.

items.title: Spotify Launches Free Streaming Option in South Korea
items.summary: Spotify has started offering a free streaming tier in South Korea, expanding its service accessibility in the region.

items.title: Elon Musk's X Returns to Brazil After Month-Long Suspension
items.summary: The social media platform X is back in Brazil after being inaccessible for over a month, having complied with all judicial demands following a clash between Elon Musk and a Brazilian justice.

items.title: Space Startups See Funding Growth Amid Investor Bet on Capex Ventures
items.summary: A report indicates that space startups are experiencing increased funding as investors bet on capex-intensive ventures, signaling renewed confidence in the sector.

items.title: Northvolt Head of Main Plant to Step Down
items.summary: Battery maker Northvolt announced that the head of its main plant will step down, marking a leadership change at the company.

items.title: American Water Cyberattack Highlights Critical Infrastructure Risks
items.summary: A continuing cyberattack on American Water, the nation's largest regulated water utility, has renewed focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure.

items.title: Dell PC Business Under New Leadership as Jeff Clarke Takes Over
items.summary: Jeff Clarke is taking over Dell's PC business as the company aims to reverse years of drift, restore the XPS halo, and compete in a market where fundamentals still win.

items.title: Hyundai's Atlas Robot Debuts at World Cup
items.summary: Hyundai's Atlas robot made its debut on the biggest stage of all—the World Cup, showcasing advancements in robotics technology.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Quantum Computer Using Standard Silicon
items.summary: Quantum Motion has deployed the first full-stack quantum computer built using standard silicon chip fabrication, a milestone that could accelerate scalable quantum systems.
